choice: Person carries: display name, date of birth, and a marital/partner link between the two adults. Birth date (not just age) is the single source for current age, the statutory RMD age (73), and SSA claiming age — derive everything else from it. Model the relationship as an explicit spouse/partner link on the household (max two adults for v1) because filing status and SSA survivor benefits depend on it. Do NOT carry sex/gender: end-ages are user-set per person (ae3a6a6a), not drawn from a mortality table, so gender has no engine input in v1.
rationale: RMD age and SSA claiming-age adjustments (701e81b4, fe56f1d6) are both functions of a person's age at a calendar step, which is only well-defined from a birth date; storing age alone drifts as the projection advances. The spouse link is the prerequisite for survivor-benefit handling the SSA engine already assumes. Gender would only earn its place if mortality were stochastic, which it isn't in v1.
